text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [0] 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Beth. And I'm Phil, do you believe there's life on other planets?
zh: [1] 我是Beth。我是Phil,你相信其他星球上有生命吗?

text: Well, there's a lot of other planets, so yeah, I think there must be.
zh: [2] 嗯,宇宙中有那么多其他行星,所以我觉得肯定有生命存在。

text: Yeah, I agree. Well, it's a question that interests American astrophysicist Adam Frank,
zh: [3] 对,我同意。这也是美国天体物理学家亚当·弗兰克(Adam Frank)感兴趣的问题,

text: who discussed it with his colleague Gavin Schmidt, director of NASA's Institute for Space Studies.
zh: [4] 他曾就此与同事加文·施密特(Gavin Schmidt)讨论过,后者是美国宇航局空间研究所所长。

text: Here, Adam recalls their conversation for BBC World Service programme CrowdScience.
zh: [5] 下面是亚当在BBC世界服务节目《CrowdScience》中回忆当时的对话。

text: We know that there's been no other civilisation on Earth and he stopped me and said, how do you know that?
zh: [6] 我们说地球上从未有过其他文明,他打断我说,你怎么知道的?

text: And my jaw just dropped down to the floor. Adam's jaw dropped, an idiom for when someone looks shocked and surprised.
zh: [7] 我当时惊讶得下巴都掉了。Adam's jaw dropped(下巴掉了)是一个习语,用来形容某人看起来非常震惊和惊讶。

text: Could there have been a technologically advanced civilisation before us here on Earth?
zh: [8] 在我们之前,地球上是否曾存在过科技高度发达的文明?

text: It may sound weird, but this idea has a scientific
zh: [9] 这听起来可能很奇怪,但这个想法确实有一个科学

text: name. Here is Caroline Steele, presenter of BBC's CrowdScience, to explain.
zh: [10] 名称。下面请BBC《CrowdScience》节目主持人卡罗琳·斯蒂尔(Caroline Steele)来解释一下。

text: The Silurian hypothesis proposes that if there was a technologically advanced civilisation
zh: [11] 志留纪假说(Silurian hypothesis)提出,如果几亿年前曾存在过科技高度发达的文明,

text: hundreds of millions of years ago, we wouldn't be able to find traces of it.
zh: [12] 我们可能根本找不到它存在过的痕迹。

text: A hypothesis is an idea which explains how something happens and can be tested to find out if it's correct.
zh: [13] 假说(hypothesis)是一种用来解释某事发生原因的想法,它可以被验证以判断是否正确。

text: In this episode, we'll be discussing the Silurian hypothesis,
zh: [14] 在这一集里,我们将讨论志留纪假说,

text: the idea that a technologically advanced civilization existed before us on Earth,
zh: [15] 也就是在我们之前地球上曾存在过科技发达文明的想法,

text: but vanished without leaving a trace. As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and idioms,
zh: [16] 但这个文明消失得无影无踪。像往常一样,我们会学习一些实用的新单词和习语,

text: and remember, you'll find all the vocabulary on our website, BBC Learning English .com
zh: [17] 记住,你可以在我们的网站BBC Learning English .com上找到所有词汇。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Phil.
zh: [18] 不过首先,Phil,我有个问题要问你。

text: Human civilisation is thousands of years old, but the planet itself is much older.
zh: [19] 人类文明只有几千年的历史,但地球本身要古老得多。

text: So how old do scientists think the Earth is?
zh: [20] 那么科学家认为地球的年龄有多大呢?

text: it a three and a half billion years, b four and a half billion years, or c five and a half billion years?
zh: [21] a. 三十五亿年,b. 四十五亿年,还是c. 五十五亿年?

text: I'm going to go c five and a half billion years.
zh: [22] 我选c,五十五亿年。

text: OK, well, we will find the answer out later in the programme.
zh: [23] 好的,我们会在节目稍后揭晓答案。

text: To believe a technologically advanced civilisation existed on Earth hundreds of millions of years ago,
zh: [24] 要相信几亿年前地球上曾存在科技高度发达的文明,

text: most people would probably want evidence.
zh: [25] 大多数人可能都想要证据。

text: We have evidence about other species from the past, such as the dinosaurs, from fossils –
zh: [26] 我们通过化石了解到过去其他物种的存在,比如恐龙——

text: the remains of prehistoric plants or animals that have been preserved in rock for a very long time.
zh: [27] 化石是史前动植物遗骸,在岩石中被长期保存下来。

text: But according to Adam Frank, fossils won't help prove the Silurian hypothesis,
zh: [28] 但据亚当·弗兰克说,化石并不能帮助证明志留纪假说,

text: as he explains here to BBC World Service programme CrowdScience.
zh: [29] 他在BBC世界服务节目《CrowdScience》中这样解释道。

text: Most things are not fossilised. It's only a tiny fraction of Earth's life that has ever become fossilised.
zh: [30] 大多数生物都没有变成化石。地球上曾经存在过的生命,只有极小一部分最终形成了化石。

text: So imagine that you have a 10 ,000 year long civilisation,
zh: [31] 所以想象一下,如果有一个持续一万年的文明,

text: which is a blink of the eye for geology, that's too short to really create a lot of fossils.
zh: [32] 这在地质学上不过是一眨眼的功夫,时间太短,根本不足以留下大量化石。

text: Adam argues that only a tiny fraction, meaning a very small amount, of life on Earth has turned into fossil.
zh: [33] 亚当认为,地球生命中只有极小一部分,也就是很少的一部分,变成了化石。

text: In geological time, even a 10 ,000 year old civilisation
zh: [34] 在地质时间尺度上,即便是一个存在一万年的文明,

text: is the blink of an eye, an idiom meaning a very short period of time.
zh: [35] 也只是“the blink of an eye”(一眨眼),这个习语的意思是极短的时间。

text: In other words, older civilisations might have existed on Earth, but not for long enough to leave fossilised evidence.
zh: [36] 换句话说,更古老的文明可能确实存在过,但存在的时间不够长,无法留下化石证据。

text: However, not everyone is convinced by Adam's ideas.
zh: [37] 然而,并不是所有人都被亚当的观点说服。

text: Evidence of our own civilisation, including plastics and man -made materials like
zh: [38] 我们当今文明留下的证据,包括塑料和混凝土等人造材料,

text: concrete, is already being layered into the Earth's crust, and these are going to last a very long time.
zh: [39] 已经在层层堆积进入地壳,而且这些痕迹会存在很长很长时间。

text: So surely a technologically advanced civilisation from prehistory would have left similar marks.
zh: [40] 那么,史前如果真的存在过科技发达的文明,应该也会留下类似的痕迹才对。

text: Well, here's Adam Frank again answering these objections on BBC World Service programme CrowdScience.
zh: [41] 我们再次请亚当·弗兰克在BBC世界服务节目《CrowdScience》中回应这些质疑。

text: What happens is somebody else will pick up on their work, either affirming it and showing new evidence for it or pushing back on it.
zh: [42] 通常的情况是,其他人会接续他们的研究,要么提供新证据加以证实,要么提出反驳。

text: And once you get to like 20, 30, 40 papers, then you have a consensus.
zh: [43] 等积累到大约二三十甚至四十篇论文时,大家就会形成共识。

text: You're like, OK, we've really, really looked at this, and now we don't.
zh: [44] 那时你会觉得,好吧,我们确实已经充分研究过这个问题了,不再需要继续争论了。

text: Adam welcomes new evidence, even evidence which contradicts his ideas,
zh: [45] 亚当欢迎新的证据,哪怕是与他观点相悖的证据,

text: as part of the scientific method needed to prove a hypothesis right or wrong.
zh: [46] 因为这正是验证一个假说对错所需的科学方法的一部分。

text: It's how scientists form a consensus, meaning a general agreement about the issue.
zh: [47] 这正是科学家们形成共识(consensus,即普遍认同)的方式。

text: Personally, I think the idea of prehistoric civilisations on Earth is so mysterious,
zh: [48] 我个人认为,史前文明存在于地球上的这个想法实在太神秘了,

text: it's OK to keep an open mind. Now, isn't it time you revealed the answer to your question, Beth?
zh: [49] 保持开放的心态未尝不可。Beth,现在是不是该公布你那个问题的答案了?

text: I think it is. I asked you how old do scientists think the Earth is.
zh: [50] 我想是的。我之前问你,科学家认为地球有多古老。

text: You said five and a half billion years and I'm afraid you were wrong, Phil.
zh: [51] 你说是五十五亿年,恐怕你答错了,Phil。

text: It's four and a half billion years. OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt,
zh: [52] 正确答案是四十五亿年。好了,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,

text: starting with the idiom my jaw dropped, which is used to say someone looks very shocked and surprised.
zh: [53] 首先是习语my jaw dropped(下巴都掉了),用来形容某人看起来非常震惊和惊讶。

text: A hypothesis is a suggestion that's proposed to explain something, which can then be tested to see if it's true.
zh: [54] Hypothesis(假说)是为解释某事而提出的一种设想,之后可以通过检验来判断其真伪。

text: Fossils are the remains of prehistoric plants or animals that have been preserved in rock for a very long time.
zh: [55] Fossils(化石)是史前动植物的遗骸,在岩石中被长期保存下来。

text: A tiny fraction of something is a very small amount of it.
zh: [56] a tiny fraction of something 指的是某物中极小的一部分。

text: The idiom the blink of an eye means a very short period of time.
zh: [57] 习语the blink of an eye(一眨眼)意思是极短的时间。

text: And finally, a consensus is a general agreement.
zh: [58] 最后,consensus(共识)指的是普遍的一致意见。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you're keen for more, then head over to our website bbclearningenglish
zh: [59] 我们的六分钟又到时间了,如果你还想了解更多,欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish

text: .com where you'll find a worksheet and a quiz for this episode.
zh: [60] .com,那里有这一集的练习题和小测验。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's Goodbye.
zh: [61] 我们下次再见,现在先说再见。

text: Six Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: [62] 《六分钟英语》,来自bbclearningenglish.com。
